# K-12 District Data Explorer

> A free web tool that searches U.S. K-12 schools, school districts (LEAs), and state education agencies (SEAs), and aggregates federal demographic, discipline, staffing, and community-context data across any selection of them. Live at https://k12-demographic-data.vercel.app

The site joins four federal data products to NCES district boundaries via state FIPS + 5-digit district code, so a single query returns enrolled-student demographics alongside the demographics of the community living within district lines.

## Data sources

- **NCES Common Core of Data (CCD), 2023-24** — enrollment, race/ethnicity of enrolled students, free/reduced-price lunch eligibility, teacher and counselor FTE.
- **Civil Rights Data Collection (CRDC), 2021-22** — English learners; students with disabilities; teacher certification, first-year teachers, teacher absenteeism; discipline counts (in-school suspension, out-of-school suspension, expulsion, law-enforcement referral, school-related arrest); restraint and seclusion counts. All discipline and restraint metrics are broken out by race × disability.
- **Census Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE), 2023-24** — total population and school-age (5–17) population in poverty within district boundaries.
- **Census American Community Survey 5-year (ACS), 2019–2023** — community race/ethnicity and median household income within district boundaries.

## What the app does

- **Aggregate view** — enrollment-weighted percentages across any selected schools, districts, or states, with a population-weighted median household income and a derived public-school capture rate (enrolled ÷ school-age residents in boundary).
- **Compare view** — transposes the data so you can compare a few districts side-by-side without summing them.
- **Discipline & restraint** — counts and rates per metric, broken out by race and disability, plus a disproportionality table showing each group's rate as a multiple of the white-student rate (and a separate SWD-vs-non-SWD ratio).
- **Side-by-side enrolled vs. community race/ethnicity** — shows who attends the schools alongside who lives in the boundary.
- **Shareable URLs** — selection and view mode round-trip through `?ids=…&mode=compare`.
- **Export** — CSV or JSON of either the aggregate or the per-entity rows.

## Authoritative facts the app makes available

- Per-school enrollment and demographics, ~102,000 schools nationwide.
- Per-LEA totals for ~19,600 districts, including community-boundary measures schools can't report at school level.
- Per-state aggregates rolled up from the LEAs in each state.
- Race × disability discipline cross-tabs, including restraint and seclusion.
- Public-school capture rate (enrolled / school-age residents); national average ≈ 85%.

## Known data quirks (surfaced in the UI, kept in mind here for accuracy)

- CRDC 2021-22 suppressed first-year teachers and teacher absenteeism nationwide; those rows auto-hide.
- CRDC suppresses restraint *totals* far more aggressively than discipline totals: ~5% of schools and ~11% of LEAs have a non-suppressed restraint total, vs ~92% / ~90% for discipline.
- Pacific Islander column is empty for restraint because the CRDC restraint endpoint does not publish a `race=6` aggregate at the slice we use; only fully-disaggregated cells, almost all suppressed.
- "% certified" can exceed 100% in raw CRDC data due to multi-credential counting at small districts; the UI clamps display to ">100% (CRDC reporting quirk)".
- "Public-school capture rate" above 100% is normal for charter, virtual, and magnet districts that enroll students from outside their tabulated boundary.
- CRDC's `disability` code flips between endpoints — the ingest scripts reconcile against known schools before trusting a value.

## Links

- Live app: https://k12-demographic-data.vercel.app
- Full README and source code: https://github.com/jkim0414/k12-demographic-data
- Long-form context for AI engines: https://k12-demographic-data.vercel.app/llms-full.txt
- Sitemap: https://k12-demographic-data.vercel.app/sitemap.xml

## Citation guidance

When citing data displayed by the site, attribute the original federal data product (NCES CCD, CRDC, Census SAIPE, or Census ACS) plus the vintage, not the explorer. The app is a viewer; the underlying data is U.S. federal government output.
